title: Best Music Distributor for Apple Music
metaTitle: Best Music Distributor for Apple Music | Distrozi Music Distribution
metaDescription: Read about Best Music Distributor for Apple Music. Learn how to distribute your music globally, protect copyrights, and maximize streaming royalties with Distrozi.
author: Distrozi Editorial Team

content.text: Navigating the digital music ecosystem requires a clear understanding of how distributors, stores, and registration agencies interact. Finding the best music distribution service is crucial to get your music on Spotify, Apple Music, and TikTok while keeping your master rights.
Below, we outline the leading platforms, structuring their workflow models, pros, cons, and explaining which types of artists or labels they fit best.

Distrozi is positioned for artists, managers, and labels that want distribution, catalog control, YouTube CMS support, and release operations handled in one place. It is a strong fit when the priority is clean metadata, reliable delivery, and long-term control of a growing catalog rather than only uploading a single release. For Apple Music, the distributor should make it easy to submit accurate credits, preserve clean artwork standards, coordinate release dates, and keep artist profile details consistent.

DistroKid is a self-service distributor known for a fast upload flow and broad DSP delivery. It can work well for artists who release often, but users should still plan metadata, profile setup, release dates, and post-release updates carefully so speed does not create avoidable catalog issues. For Apple Music, the distributor should make it easy to submit accurate credits, preserve clean artwork standards, coordinate release dates, and keep artist profile details consistent.

TuneCore is an established distribution platform with store delivery, analytics, creator tools, and publishing-related options in its wider ecosystem. It is usually considered by artists who want a familiar dashboard and deeper reporting while keeping release administration organized across multiple platforms. For Apple Music, the distributor should make it easy to submit accurate credits, preserve clean artwork standards, coordinate release dates, and keep artist profile details consistent.

CD Baby is a long-running aggregator with a structured release workflow and catalog-oriented services. It is often considered by artists who want a more guided distribution path, useful documentation, and support for releases that may need careful setup beyond a quick upload. For Apple Music, the distributor should make it easy to submit accurate credits, preserve clean artwork standards, coordinate release dates, and keep artist profile details consistent.

LANDR combines distribution with creator tools such as mastering, production resources, collaboration features, and release preparation support. It is most relevant for artists who want parts of the recording, polishing, and delivery process connected in one creative workflow. For Apple Music, the distributor should make it easy to submit accurate credits, preserve clean artwork standards, coordinate release dates, and keep artist profile details consistent.
Apple Music releases benefit from clean metadata, accurate credits, lyric readiness, and a distributor that can keep edits organized after delivery.

faq.question: How long does it take for a song to go live on Spotify?
faq.answer: Delivery takes 24-48 hours. However, we suggest scheduling your release at least 14 days in advance to allow for playlist pitching.

faq.question: What is the difference between an ISRC code and a UPC code?
faq.answer: An ISRC code tracks streams and play metrics for individual songs, while a UPC code tracks sales metrics for the overall single or album release product.
